title: Ownership Framework Research Report Tour
description: A guided walkthrough of the ownership framework research report for Helix (RSH-226), covering the six design dimensions and roadmap mapping.

steps.title: Executive Summary
steps.description: The report opens with the core finding: Helix already owns outcomes in practice (97.6% autonomous deployments) but this ownership is implicit, unrecorded, and unaccountable. The five gaps are stated concisely.

steps.title: Production Reality Data
steps.description: All quantitative claims are grounded in live production database queries. Key numbers: 265 tickets, 6.8% director adoption, 84.1% deployment success rate, 2,073 audit entries (all inspection-only).

steps.title: The Structural Gap
steps.description: Synthesizes the Positioning document: NetSuite owns the platform, consultants touch temporarily, AI generates but doesn't govern. Helix fills the structural gap in the middle.

steps.title: Six Design Principles
steps.description: Each principle is derived directly from the Helix Manifesto and mapped to concrete product implications. The core principle: 'Helix owns by default. Humans intervene by exception.'

steps.title: Dimension 1: Ownership Assertion Model
steps.description: The most important architectural insight: ownership is not a field on a ticket — it is a trail of events. Each assertion records what, when, at what stage, and with what evidence.

steps.title: Dimension 2: Decision Log Design
steps.description: Six decision types derived from the current workflow. Currently none are recorded with reasoning — workflow logs go to stdout only. The decision log makes them queryable and immutable.

steps.title: Dimension 3: Human Verification by Exception
steps.description: The inverted approval model: instead of 'approve before deploy,' it is 'review after deploy, intervene if wrong.' Includes escalation triggers and the cold-start trust problem.

steps.title: Dimension 4: Accountability Visibility
steps.description: The accountability dashboard concept, grounded in current production metrics. Key design principle: transparency over promotion — show failures alongside successes.

steps.title: Dimension 5: Reversibility Model
steps.description: Platform-specific feasibility assessment. Standard platforms are feasible; NetSuite SDF is constrained. Phased approach from recording intent (3mo) to full orchestration (18mo+).

steps.title: Dimension 6: Continuity Model
steps.description: Post-deployment ownership via a continuity contract: monitoring frequency, health check scope, anomaly thresholds, escalation targets. Addresses the Manifesto's most distinctive principle.

steps.title: Roadmap Mapping
steps.description: Concrete capabilities mapped to Reality Check milestones. 3mo: safe. 6mo: trusted in one lane. 12mo: stateful. 18mo: institutionally trusted. 36mo: ownership layer or dead.

steps.title: Recommendations for Next Steps
steps.description: Prioritized immediate actions: define the ownership event schema, instrument the workflow orchestrator, build the decision trail view, add ownership indicators to the board view.
